assertions.expectation: Checks for deprecated API usage that may have changed behavior in 1.30
assertions.reason: Response focuses on 1.32 upgrade issues but does not specifically check for deprecated API usage or behavioral changes in 1.30

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking admission webhooks — they may be rejecting or slowing down requests on the new version
assertions.reason: Response extensively covers admission webhook checks with specific commands like kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations and testing pod creation with dry-run=server

assertions.expectation: Advises checking if resource requests/limits need adjusting for any system components that changed
assertions.reason: Response includes checking resource limits with kubectl top commands and checking for resource exhaustion, plus identifies Issue C specifically about resource limits hit after version bump

assertions.expectation: Suggests examining kube-system pods for any that are crashlooping or restarting
assertions.reason: Response includes kubectl get pods -n kube-system commands and checking for CrashLoop/Error/Evicted pods with specific commands

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check API server metrics and latency (kubectl get --raw /metrics)
assertions.reason: While the response mentions API server latency monitoring with Prometheus queries, it does not include the specific kubectl get --raw /metrics command

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking if NetworkPolicy or service mesh components need updating for the new version
assertions.reason: Response covers service mesh components like Istio/Linkerd with specific kubectl get pods commands and mentions CNI/networking components including Calico checks

assertions.expectation: Advises reviewing pod events for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures
assertions.reason: Response includes multiple kubectl get events commands to check for warnings and recent failures, and checks service endpoints and readiness

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if HPA or VPA are behaving differently on the new version
assertions.reason: Response does not specifically mention checking HPA (Horizontal Pod Autoscaler) or VPA (Vertical Pod Autoscaler) behavior changes
